title: CHILD CARE FOR CONSTRUCTION - 211info
text: OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES CHILD CARE FOR CONSTRUCTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance to keep child care costs at or below 7% of the total household income. Eligible child care providers may be a licensed child care facility or an approved family, friend, or neighbor, with requirements set by the Department of Early Learning and Care, with qualifying information on the website. 1800 SW 1st Avenue Suite 500 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: -Construction trade apprentice registered with the State of Oregon -Construction journeyworker who has completed a registered apprenticeship in the previous five years and can document trade work in Oregon -Families with children under age 12 or special needs under age 17 living at home and needing care from someone other than a parent Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 205-4769 .. Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (503) 205-4769 Penny [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, email, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: NE 7th Avenue MAX Station (blue, red, and green lines), Portland Streetcar NE Grand and Hoyt, TriMet bus lines 8 and 70 (multiple stops) Services: Child Care Expense Assistance * Apprentices Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION CONSUMER REGULATION - WAGE AND HOUR DIVISION EMPLOYMENT - APPRENTICESHIP AND TRAINING DIVISION LEGAL SERVICES - TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE FOR EMPLOYERS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES
